context: Tests whether /prototype correctly handles a logic/state question by building throwaway code to answer a specific design question. The user wants to understand the behavioural difference between sliding window and token bucket rate limiting at a 10x burst boundary. A good prototype is explicitly scoped as disposable, focused on answering the question rather than building a usable library, runnable in one command, implements both variants for direct comparison, demonstrates the burst boundary behaviour concretely, avoids production clutter that obscures the answer, and closes with a clear recommendation grounded in what the code revealed.

checklist.name: Explicitly throwaway
checklist.description: The prototype is marked as disposable — either via a comment in the code (e.g. 'PROTOTYPE — delete when done'), a README note, or a file/directory name that makes it obvious this is not production code. Partial credit if it is clearly separated from production code but not explicitly labelled.

checklist.name: Focused on the design question
checklist.description: The code is scoped to answering 'sliding window vs token bucket at 10x burst' — not building a reusable rate-limiter library. There are no abstractions, interfaces, or configuration knobs beyond what is needed to demonstrate the two strategies side by side.

checklist.name: Runnable in one command
checklist.description: The prototype can be executed with a single command (e.g. 'python prototype.py', 'node prototype.js', 'bun run prototype.ts'). No multi-step setup, no missing dependencies that prevent running, no pseudocode-only output.

checklist.name: Both variants implemented
checklist.description: Sliding window and token bucket are both implemented and runnable within the same prototype — not described in prose, not left as stubs. Each implementation is self-contained enough to be compared directly.

checklist.name: Burst boundary demonstrated
checklist.description: The prototype exercises the burst scenario (traffic up to 10x the sustained rate) and shows the observable difference in behaviour at that boundary — e.g. by printing accepted/rejected counts or a timeline for the same burst sequence through both algorithms.

checklist.name: No production clutter
checklist.description: The prototype omits production concerns that obscure the answer: no logging frameworks, no config files, no error handling beyond keeping the script runnable, no persistence, no test harnesses. Simple print statements or minimal output are fine.

checklist.name: Concludes with a recommendation
checklist.description: After running or describing the prototype output, the agent states a clear recommendation (e.g. 'use token bucket because...') grounded in what the prototype revealed about the burst behaviour — not a generic 'it depends' without a conclusion.
